title: New California law requires human teachers, not AI
description: California's new law ensures K-12 students are taught by human teachers, not artificial intelligence.
content:
California is drawing a line in the digital sand.
Starting 2027, public schools in the Golden State can’t have AI teachers for K-12 students.

title: Appeals Court Warns Bankruptcy Lawyer Over Alleged AI Citations
description: A federal appeals court admonished, but declined to sanction, an attorney for a brief containing what one judge called “an astonishing number” of citations that were incorrect or possibly hallucinated by artificial intelligence.

title: Rising AI Demand Drives Surge in Data Center Water Consumption, Reports Indicate
description: Analyze the environmental cost of Artificial Intelligence (AI) as data center water consumption spikes to unprecedented levels in the U.S.
content:

title: US judge warns Justice Department about AI use in immigration case
description: A federal judge in Michigan on Thursday cautioned the U.S. ​Justice Department about the risks associated with generative artificial intelligence after finding that a government filing ‌cited a case that the court could not locate.

title: MLB effectively outlaws use of AI on dugout iPads during games
description: Major League Baseball banned artificial intelligence tools on iPads during games after teams were building custom apps to help influence in-game strategy.
content:

title: AI humanoid robotics company sets up shop in Fremont
description: A robotics company has set up shop in Fremont, bringing dozens of jobs and robot-related artificial intelligence technologies and to the East Bay.
content:
FREMONT — Robotics company Agility expects to bring jobs and robot-related artificial intelligence technologies to Fremont’s Ardenwood neighborhood with its new research center.

title: Humanoid robots and smart homes put AI centre stage at VivaTech 2026 in Paris

title: US Navy Chasing Data, AI for 'AI-First' Fleet
description: Artificial intelligence will be used across naval operations to accelerate the speed of information and data gathering.
content:
The US Navy wants to build a new "AI-first" fleet, and it's betting that better data, faster technology adoption, and more tech-savvy sailors will help make it happen.
